title: Wire verify_module_integrity() into orchestrator startup for self-tamper detection
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 — LLM03/LLM10 supply-chain & model/script integrity; SLSA provenance principles. verify_module_integrity() exists in security_utils.py but has zero callers.
description: security_utils.py:1021 defines a complete SHA-256 module-integrity verifier, but grep shows it is never called anywhere. The self-evolving Prometheus loop rewrites its own scripts, so an injected or corrupted delegate.py/security_utils.py would execute silently. Calling the integrity check at startup turns an existing-but-dormant control into an active tamper tripwire (defense for a system that edits its own code).
proposed_changes: Call `verify_module_integrity()` once at orchestrate.py main() entry and at mcp_server.py startup. On status 'degraded' (mismatches/missing), emit a roro event + audit_log_security_event(severity='critical') and require an explicit CTO_INTEGRITY_OVERRIDE=true env var to continue (otherwise abort). After legitimate Prometheus self-edits, run `python3 security_utils.py security-check --update-manifest` to re-baseline so the manifest stays current. Add the resulting .cto/security/module-hashes.json to .gitignore-awareness so it is treated as local state.
